title: Level 229

{{Class Ψ|entity=Mental Hazards; Rare Entity Presence|hexagon=Ψ}}
Level 229 is the 230th discovered level of the Backrooms.
== Description ==
{{Blurimage|file=Image444-2.png|caption=An image of Level 229. A rusty old car is present in the photo taken by [REDACTED]. [REDACTED] is now residing in Level 4.}}{{Blurimage|file=Image444-3.png|caption=A dilapidated structure within Level 229. It is either a warehouse or a nuclear shelter, according to [REDACTED].}} Level 229 is an infinite conglomeration of random abandoned and dilapidated structures and objects in the middle of an empty plain at night, usually having unnatural, distorted architectural design in some ways. These structures are entirely random as they are not particularly relevant to each other, excluding the fact that they are commonly seen. Because of the confusing structure and the non-Euclidean nature of the level, there is an astronomically rare chance to meet other wanderers unless one enters at the same location as another person. It is also very easy to get lost within the level if one does not bring a tool used for keeping track of where they go. However, because of its geological properties, encountering entities is also quite uncommon. It is still advised to traverse carefully, but there is no constant danger. The main dangers are related to psychology, where staying on this level for extended periods can harm one's mental stability, ultimately resulting in traumatic recalls (PTSD) or even incurable sanity in more extreme cases. The danger is also present in the lack of naturally present beneficial supplies like [[Object 1|Almond Water]] that spawn on the level. The lack of useful resources or necessities can be particularly dangerous because of said properties, so it is advised to be very resourceful with one's supplies. Strangely, the sky does not exhibit any stars or moon, thus making it look entirely black, though there are minimal amounts of light sources on the level itself. It is thought to have a connection to [[The Void]] itself, but this is most likely a myth. This heftily reduces visibility, and it is recommended to use any tool at one's disposal to see clearly and recognize any threat that may come. Light is also useful for scouting for naturally-spawned supplies. == Entities ==
Entities are present in Level 229; however, they are rarely sighted. Some confirmed entities that reside on the level include [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] and [[Entity 3|Smilers]] since they hunt more efficiently in dark environments. One instance of a [[Entity 44|Silver Slime]] was documented, found on a rusted generator inside a warehouse, indicating a potential presence of the entity within the level. There are also [[Phenomenon 35|victims of the Wretched Cycle]] present, but this is mostly due to the mental effects of this level. There have also been reported sightings of shadowy figures, but this is unconfirmed and is most likely other humans, which, with the environment of the level, appears dark because of the apparent lack of light sources. == Colonies and Settlements ==
Due to the nature of the level, no colonies exist in the present moment. However, there have been a few attempts at establishing temporary and small research colonies by the [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] and the [[The Kauer Research Organization|K.R.O]]. These colonies had to be disbanded because of the hazards present within the level and the high risk of getting lost. Both organizations have decided to keep information about the missing colonies completely confidential from the public and threatens any surviving members of these colonies not to pass any information about their mission or experience whatsoever. Not doing so may lead to dire consequences. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances ===
* Opening and going through a random door in [[Level 6|Level 6]] that is supposedly darker on the other side can lead one here. * Dark outdoor levels can transition to Level 229 if the wanderer goes far enough. This can be noticed by a gradual disappearance of stars or light sources, however, it depends on whether said level has stars or not. If not, then the transition can go temporarily unnoticed. === Exits ===
Exiting Level 229 is quite difficult because of the inconsistent architecture and its infinite nature, but there have been some alleged exits taken from recovered video recordings from wanderers: * If more buildings, typically representing suburban homes and being noticeably less rusty, gradually start to appear, the level is slowly transitioning into [[Level 9]]. * If a modern road or infrastructure is present, walking down that path might no-clip one to [[Level 777]]. {{Collapse|num=2|initial=

|body=
''The Vanity Of Absolute Faith''

Act 0 - PROLOGUE Unwavering Optimism or Brutal Realism?
A dilemma that exists in the minds of every single human being. Being hopeful is a naive expression of the human mind, the false reminder that everything will go in favor of the individual in the end. Being pessimistic is the root of failure, waiting for the total surrender of morale and soon all possible paths for salvation. Something very common in a dimension so vastly different from normality, yet so similar—a juxtaposition between absolute terror beyond the mind's comprehension and simple familiarity. The individual experiences a sense of confusion, a sense of terror, an insatiable desire for knowledge, for the mysteries of infinity expand so much beyond. But why, or how? Why was this meaningless universe, an Ouroboros of suffering and death created? The weight of our problems or mere curiosity tends to carry us down, and to counter this, our minds create a false scenario of relief and try their hardest to reach that goal, to reach a happy conclusion, even when nothing can be done. Religion is one example of this. What, or perhaps, who created such a complex network of stars and galaxies and put life in the middle of it all? Or, perhaps, the more important question of why? Is it truly just a fine game of nil, is it to suffer, or is it to achieve peace and tranquility? Humans are so desperate to know things they should not and can not that they imagine divine beings, higher entities beyond the plains of their known existence. On the other hand, too much anxiety can usually lead one to feel hopeless or despair, as if any of their attempts to benefit themselves or others will all be worthless, futile attempts in the end. This can prevent any growth or improvement in the self. So what's the solution? Or is there none?

Act I - ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
"Even in Hell, the flower of Hope can bloom." That is something I remind myself day by day when venturing out into this mysterious hellhole of infinite structures and creatures. These new locations I wake up to every single minute, adapting and venturing, eventually seeing a point in all of this constant suffering. This level was no different. It was quite the same with any others I have went through, whether it be dark, confusing halls that span for an indefinite amount packed with various creatures, most of them wanting to rip my head off or forests at night, the heavy vegetation trapping me in their wicked vines, slowly bleeding me out. Except this level was a bit different—the silence of it was deafening. It was a pretty good sign to me at first, seeing as there were no terrifying, gross monsters chasing me when I had just no-clipped into this realm... But the sky... the sky was empty. It wasn't day or night to me—rather a reminder that the Backrooms is not merely a new dimension, it was a purgatory itself. Albeit, I kept walking on. And on. And on, looking at the old factories and cars scattered around the place, and even some recollections of what I used to recognize as Ohio, USA. For two days straight, I ventured into the unknown once again. But I've yet to see anything. It was so vast, so incredibly enormous, that seeing life was like a miracle, whether it be an entity or a fellow human. I was hungry and thirsty. I couldn't see where I was going, and I was confused. I haven't seen a single bottle of water or even a single breadcrumb. I was getting desperate. I was suddenly struck with a realization: the level wanted me to suffer, it didn't want me to die an easy death, and it could've done so so easily. I didn't want to accept it, but for the first time, I knew that God wasn't always on my side. The level was the setting, and I was simply a doomed character. I took a final sip of the bottle of Almond Water I've used to sustain me for so long and lay down on the ground, waiting for my demise.

Act II - EVEN IN DEATH...
The folly of man,
The puppets on the stage
Wanders tirelessly out of sadness or rage
But again; being man
They fall once or twice or thrice a chance!
But even in certain doom
And even amid foggy gloom
And in death, their very last breath
They still look at the light
And they say that it is bright
How silly, how funny
How naive their minds can be
One should know when they've lost
What is hope in a world of ''misery''?
}}
